title: buildah does not work on fargate after recent fargate scheduled change
body:
Currently encountering error "Error during unshare(CLONE_NEWUSER): No space left on device" when running commands like `buildah login` and `buildah images` inside fargate pods.

We tried running command `cat /proc/sys/user/max_user_namespaces` which returns "0" as output. Based on the AWS support response, user namespace creation is disabled at kernel level on current fargate platform. 

We have set env variable BUILDAH_ISOLATION=chroot 

Buildah version:  1.33.14
OS/Arch: linux/amd64
EKS cluster version: v1.35

Seems like buildah will attempt(CLONE_NEWUSER) which may require CAP_SYS_ADMIN capability but this is not supported by fargate. 

Previously we had no issues but this seems to happen only recently this week which coincides with the scheduled fargate change by AWS. 

Was wondering if there is any workaround or if anyone is encountering the same issue

comments.body:
If you have user namespaces disabled you cannot run buildah rootless.

If you run as root with all capabilities, i.e. having CAP_SYS_ADMIN then I think it should work fine without a user namespace.
